definitions.text: To rest in a horizontal position on a surface.
examples: The book lies on the table; the snow lies on the roof; he lies in his coffin.
Under an oak were lying asleep a pair of shepherds.

definitions.text: The terrain and conditions surrounding the ball before it is struck.
The terrain and conditions surrounding the disc before it is thrown.
The position of a fetus in the womb.
To give false information intentionally with intent to deceive.
examples: When Pinocchio lies, his nose grows.
If you are found to have lied in court, you could face a penalty.

definitions.text: To convey a false image or impression.
An intentionally false statement; an intentional falsehood.
examples: I knew he was telling a lie by his facial expression.

definitions.text: A statement intended to deceive, even if literally true.
